todos.text: Fertilize Sally Holmes and geranium
Clean fountain planter
Wash fountain wall
Support Jasmine at top of the pillar post flowering
In late June / early July - light sprinkle of fertilizer for hydrangeas (Epsoma Holly tone)
Train Iceberg climbing rose canes horizontally across Side Arbor — vine eyes + soft ties now while flexible
Cut back Distictis buccinatoria (Trumpet Vine on Fountain Wall) hard to framework — create structure as you cut
Move White Bougainvillea to back of BBQ bed
Cut back existing 6ft Fatsia — take 6-8 cuttings with rooting hormone
Selective deadwood removal on all hydrangeas — dead/crossing stems only
White Stripe bougainvillea — Super Bloom HALF strength to MOIST soil. Monthly.
Diseased rose — Safer spray every 7-14 days. Do NOT feed until healthy.
Deadhead all geraniums aggressively
Cut back leggy tall geranium by half to two thirds
Move kitchen windowsill plants (Peperomia, Succulent, Mini Jade) to office once Rieger Begonias arrive
Monitor irrigation — check soil 48hrs after watering. Drop to 2x/week if still moist.
Spot plant all new plants with pots on ground first — step back to street, adjust, THEN plant
Confirm Breath of Heaven variety matches existing — check foliage color at nursery
Check nursery tag on fern in white smiley cat pot (plant stand) — ID species
Get remaining rose names from Side Arbor — varieties beyond Iceberg, Angel Face, Burst of Joy
ID David Austin rose variety in Back Rose Bed — check tag for exact name
Phormium orphan with trunk (actually a Cordyline) — find a home: BBQ bed, poolside, or large container

shopping.name: Yellow lantana for front rose bed
Ground cover for stepping stones
shopping.notes: Ground cover options: green carpet/rupturewort or blue star creeper.

shopping.name: Climbing rose
shopping.notes: Buy next year: Madame Alfred Carriere or Perfume Breeze or New Dawn

shopping.name: Tickseed or balloon flower
shopping.notes: This is replacement for the chrysanthemum

projects.title: 2026 power wash, compost and mulch
projects.notes:
Power wash: $600 
Soil, Compost and mulch: $500 
Areation: $200 (I will pay for seeds and fertilizer)
Gutter Cleaning: $150
Total (Cash): $1450 + 100 (tip) = $1550

Had Jesus Medina. Look for someone else next time. 

Compost: Fine compost with minerals . Finally used 7 cu yds 
Mulch - 6 cu yds redwood small bark
Soil - 2 cu yds 
Cost of all materials: 439.55 + 860.50 +200 (lawn fertilizer and seeds)‎ = 1500

TOTAL: $3050

projects.title: 2026 Irrigation project (bring all valves over ground)
projects.notes:
Went with Shruti’s guy David. 

Quotes below:
Affordable irrigation: 
$140 per hour 
4-6 hours 
About 6 valves 
No trees 
Service valve - 1-2 hours 

Douglas irrigation: whacko!!
$60 per hour. Bradley. Will take about 2 days 
But he didn’t inspire much confidence 

Rodrigo: 
$3900 for the project 

✅David (Shruti’s guy):
Option 1: 
Move each remaining valve overground $150 per valve 

Option 2:
Move all valves overground and clean these up so that all over ground boxes is close by. Add an irrigation shut off valve. And swap out Rodrigo valves for better quality ones. $300 per valve and will take about 2 days. $60 per valve to upgrade existing overground valves that Rodrigo installed in 2025

Went with option 2. Total: $2210
